Market concentration concerns about the Magnificent Seven (Nvidia, Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet, Meta, Tesla) controlling 30% of the S&P 500 are overblown, according to research from Elm Wealth. A comparison of two major U.S. technology ETFs reveals that VGT offers broader diversification with 320 holdings across the tech sector, while XLK is more concentrated with 71 stocks and slightly lower fees. Wedbush Securities analyst Dan Ives identified 2026 as the inflection point for AI, naming five stocks as top plays: Microsoft, Apple, Tesla, Palantir, and CrowdStrike. Alphabet is recommended as a top AI stock to buy with $5,000, offering approximately 18 shares at current prices. Investors may consider redeploying capital to faster-growing tech peers or diversifying into broader market ETFs. | IWM and QQQ offer different investment approaches: QQQ focuses on large-cap tech stocks with concentrated holdings and higher long-term returns but greater volatility, while IWM provides broader small-cap diversification across sectors with lower volatility but lower long-term growth. Amazon, Microsoft, and Apple are positioned as smart choices for cautious investors due to their established market leadership, diversified revenue streams, and long histories of earnings growth alongside their AI initiatives. | REalloys is building the only non-Chinese supply chain for rare earth magnets, critical components in defense systems, medical devices, and consumer electronics.
